description_long: This is a two-story commercial building with a weathered, vintage appearance, featuring a prominent painted advertisement for 'Ideal Hosiery' on one side. The structure is clad in aged wooden siding, with brickwork forming the foundation and steps. A large rooftop water tank, metal staircases, and various utility fixtures add to its industrial character. The front facade displays large windows and signage for 'Retail & Wholesale' and 'Ladiesmen's Hosiery,' suggesting a retail function. The surrounding area includes cracked pavement, a small shed, barrels, crates, and a streetlamp, evoking an urban streetscape. The overall look is reminiscent of early-to-mid 20th-century American city architecture, with utilitarian and slightly rundown details.
description: A weathered, two-story commercial building with vintage signage, rooftop water tank, and industrial details, set on a cracked urban pavement with barrels and a shed nearby.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rear and left side, with a metal staircase, rooftop water tank, utility pipes, and a small shed with barrels and crates.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance, large retail windows, prominent signage, brick steps, and a streetlamp.
description_view_2: Side view dominated by a large painted 'Ideal Hosiery' advertisement, rooftop tank, and external air conditioning unit.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring the full metal staircase, shed, barrels, and the back of the rooftop tank.
attribute_signage: There are two main exterior signs: a large painted wall sign on the right side reading 'IDEAL HOSIERY Fit for Today's Woman SINCE 1915 Ladies Hosiery' in yellow with black and green text, and a rooftop-mounted sign above the front facade reading 'RETAIL & WHOLESALE' in yellow and black. The front also has painted window signage for 'Ladiesmen's Hosiery.'
attribute_architectural_style: Retro industrial-commercial style with utilitarian features, exposed brick foundation, aged wood siding, metal staircases, and vintage painted advertisements, evoking early-to-mid 20th-century American urban architecture.
